title: Enable free vend mode for portal-initiated charging with tracked usage
goal: Allow portal admins/operators to enable free vend mode for portal-initiated charging so drivers do not need app activation or payment to begin charging, while preserving standard OCPP 1.6J transaction flow and full session/usage tracking in the portal.
acceptanceCriteria: A portal-configurable free vend setting exists at the chosen initial scope (recommended: site-level)
When free vend is enabled, portal-initiated charging can start without driver app activation or driver payment method
The implementation uses standard OCPP transaction flow (RemoteStartTransaction -> StartTransaction -> MeterValues -> StopTransaction) so usage is still tracked
OCPP server accepts the designated free-vend idTag only in the intended free-vend path without weakening normal driver auth globally
A Session record is still created and populated with transactionId, usage, duration, and charger/session timeline data
Free-vend sessions bill the driver $0 while preserving usage and operational reporting
Portal/reporting can distinguish free-vend sessions from normal paid sessions
Existing mobile/app-paid charging flows remain unchanged when free vend is disabled
Vendor-specific charger native free-vend config keys are treated as optional capability enhancements, not a requirement for the feature

inScope: Define free-vend product behavior for portal-initiated charging only
Add site or charger configuration field(s) for free vend enablement and designated free-vend idTag strategy
Update portal remote-start / automation flow to use free-vend mode when enabled
Update OCPP Authorize and StartTransaction handling to allow the configured free-vend idTag in a constrained, explicit way
Ensure session creation, meter tracking, and stop handling continue through the normal OCPP lifecycle
Add billing/session markers so free-vend sessions are zero-charge but still fully tracked
Expose enough metadata in portal/reporting to identify complimentary/free-vend sessions
Document optional follow-up support for charger-native vendor configuration keys via GetConfiguration/ChangeConfiguration when supported
outOfScope: A generic public no-auth charging mode for all session starts
Changing mobile driver flow to allow anonymous free charging
Vendor-by-vendor charger firmware support matrix completion in v1
Complex promotional pricing, coupons, or one-time free-session campaigns
Automatic site-wide retroactive rebilling of historical sessions
Replacing the normal OCPP StartTransaction/StopTransaction lifecycle with synthetic sessions

decisions.decision: Use software-defined free vend as the primary implementation path
decisions.rationale: OCPP 1.6J does not guarantee a universal charger-native free-vend flag across vendors. RemoteStartTransaction with an accepted idTag is the safest cross-vendor path while preserving StartTransaction-based tracking.

decisions.decision: Treat charger-native free-vend configuration keys as optional, vendor-specific follow-up
decisions.rationale: Some chargers expose keys like FreeVendActive or FreeChargingIdTag, but these are not core-spec guarantees. The feature must not depend on them.

decisions.decision: Keep free vend scoped to portal-initiated automation in v1
decisions.rationale: This avoids accidentally weakening the standard driver-initiated payment and authorization model while still solving the operator use case.

assumptions: Target chargers support RemoteStartTransaction and normal StartTransaction/StopTransaction lifecycle
The existing portal remote-start path is the correct orchestration point for free-vend initiation
The billing model can be extended to mark sessions as complimentary/free-vend without losing usage accounting
A dedicated free-vend idTag or equivalent constrained identity can be introduced safely
implementationPlan: 1. Confirm initial scope and data model: site-level freeVendEnabled plus free-vend idTag/config strategy
2. Inspect current billing/session model to determine where complimentary/free-vend marker should be stored and snapshotted
3. Add schema/API fields for free-vend configuration and session classification
4. Update portal settings UI and portal-initiated remote start flow to use free-vend mode when enabled
5. Update OCPP Authorize path to accept the designated free-vend idTag in a tightly scoped way
6. Update StartTransaction/session creation path so free-vend sessions are recorded normally and flagged appropriately
7. Update billing/reporting logic so free-vend sessions total $0 while preserving usage, energy, duration, and ops reporting
8. Add optional capability note or detection path for vendor-specific charger config keys, without making the feature depend on them
9. Run QC: typecheck, API/OCPP proof, billing proof, regression proof for normal paid flows
filesExpectedToChange: packages/api/src/lib/sessionBilling.ts or related billing integration points
docs and/or memory notes for free-vend behavior
risks: If auth bypass is implemented too broadly, free vend could unintentionally weaken normal charging authorization
If sessions are synthesized instead of relying on StartTransaction, usage tracking and billing integrity can break
Some charger firmware may have vendor-specific assumptions around idTag behavior or authorization timing
If billing logic is not explicitly updated, free-vend sessions may still accrue activation or energy charges
Portal automation and reporting can drift if free-vend classification is not snapshotted at session start
openQuestions: Should free vend be site-scoped first, or charger-scoped with optional site default?
What exact idTag strategy should be used: one global free-vend idTag, per-site idTag, or a managed pseudo-user model?
Should free-vend sessions count as revenue zero but still have notional tariff pricing captured for analytics?
Should portal manually initiated remote starts and automated starts share the same free-vend behavior contract?

proofOfWork.summary: Scoped free-vend task and mapped OCPP-standard-safe approach vs vendor-specific charger config keys before implementation.
proofOfWork.artifact: Repo scan: portal remote-start route, OCPP authorize/startTransaction handlers, billing/session touchpoints; external OCPP free-vend research summary captured in task contract.
